choices.text: Sense (sensors) + think (controllers) + act (actuators) machine. Differs from pure AI (LLMs, chess) by having physical body that interacts with world.
Accelerometer + gyroscope + magnetometer. Linear accel + angular vel + compass. Integration over time gives velocity/position (drift). Fuse with vision (VIO) for robust pose.
Forward kinematics: joints → end-effector pose (easy chain mult). Inverse kinematics: pose → joints (hard, multi-solution, no closed-form 6+ DoF). MoveIt + TRAC-IK for IK solving.
Jacobian rank loss → can't move in some direction. E.g., arm fully extended can't move along axis. Plan paths to avoid; or damped pseudoinverse for graceful degradation.

tf2 library in ROS 2: tree of frames (map → odom → base_link → sensors); buffer.lookup_transform(); URDF describes rigid links; robot_state_publisher publishes joint frames. Quaternions for orientations.

questions.explanation: tf2 + URDF + quaternions = ROS frame management.
